title: Get WSL disk as an OCI artifact
body:
[Since a few days](https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-wsl-os/pull/6) WSL disk releases are also pushed to [quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl](https://quay.io/repository/podman/machine-os-wsl?tab=tags). This PR updates `podman machine init` mechanism to download WSL disk images. The WSL disk images are now pulled from quay.io as for the rest of the providers.

Fixes #22927 and [RUN-2177](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-2177)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

Just noticed this PR as a drive-by... Does this mean in future podman-machine will be able to run any available WSL image?

Even on x86_64 Linux and aarch64 macOS (via Rosetta if an x86_64 vm).

If yes, I am really excited for this features :)

But this is probably wishful thinking on my end...

I guess it's more WSL disk image specifically designed for Windows/WSL2
@l0rd could you please add Fixes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22927 as well?

> Just noticed this PR as a drive-by... Does this mean in future podman-machine will be able to run any available WSL image?
> 
> Even on x86_64 Linux and aarch64 macOS (via Rosetta if an x86_64 vm).
> 
> If yes, I am really excited for this features :)

@ericcurtin No, unfortunately this PR doesn't fix that. It doesn't have any user-visible impact. We are just changing the way the disk image for WSL is distributed.

> @l0rd could you please add Fixes #22927 as well?

@inknos sure, I have updated the description.
@Luap99 thank you for the review. I have updated the description and the commit message as suggested.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *l0rd*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@benoitf and @containers/podman-desktop-reviewers this may have an impact on Podman Desktop because we are publishing the WSL disk image to [quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl](https://quay.io/repository/podman/machine-os-wsl?tab=tags) and we should stop doing GitHub releases after the release of Podman 5.3.
